behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_STATUE::look' (examine) handler for a statue object. When a player or actor inspects the statue the handler prepares and displays a short descriptive text and manages related object state around that presentation.

On entry it sets an info/state code (0x0208) on the statue's process/object and applies a process-exclude operation to that same object (likely to protect or alter processing while the description is presented).

It constructs a temporary string beginning with the literal "statue " (concatenated with a pid/context), calls the statue instance's Item::bark method to present that text to the viewer, frees the temporary string, returns the bark result, and then suspends.
notable_control_flow: Entry (Event 0 'U8P_STATUE::look') -> set info (0x0208) on target object/process -> process exclude on target object/process -> build temporary string 'statue ' -> call Item::bark with constructed string -> free temporary string -> preserve/return bark retval -> implies/pop sequence -> suspend/return.
The handler uses an implies/pop sequence after the Item::bark return value; this appears to be a short control/return cleanup before suspending.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of info code 0x0208 (inferred to select descriptive metadata or dialog entry but not proven).
Precise effect and scope of the 'process exclude' opcode in this context (appears to prevent reentrancy or normal processing, but details are engine-specific).

What exact data is concatenated into the temporary string beyond the literal 'statue ' (a pid or context pointer is pushed; the final human-visible text beyond the literal is inferred but not explicitly documented).

main_function.name: U8P_STATUE::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player/actor 'U8P_STATUE::look' (examine) action on a statue by setting an info/state on the statue, temporarily modifying its process handling, building a short descriptive string ('statue ...'), invoking the statue's Item::bark to present that text, cleaning up temporary resources, and returning the bark result.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — the statue is looked at or examined by the avatar/actor (the 'U8P_STATUE::look' action).

function_map.name: U8P_STATUE::look
function_map.role: Handle the U8P_STATUE::look/examine event for the statue object; prepare and present descriptive text and manage temporary state around that presentation.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item method used to present/display/speak a string from the item/process; called by STATUE::look to show the statue description.

function_map.name: U8P_STATUE::set info (opcode)
function_map.role: Engine opcode used to set an info/state code (0x0208) on a target object/process before presenting the description.

function_map.name: U8P_STATUE::process exclude (opcode)
function_map.role: Engine opcode applied to the target process/object to alter or pause normal processing while the bark occurs (likely a reentrancy or processing guard).

function_map.name: U8P_STATUE::free string (opcode)
function_map.role: Engine opcode used to deallocate the temporary string buffer created for the bark call.

external_calls.purpose: Set an info/state identifier (0x0208) on the statue object/process to select related metadata or behavior prior to presenting the description.
Temporarily exclude or modify processing for the statue process/object to avoid reentrant or undesired processing during the bark/description.
Present or emit the constructed descriptive string to the viewer (display text or play a voice/bark associated with the item).
Free the temporary string buffer allocated to build the description after Item::bark returns.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the 'U8P_STATUE::look' (examine) handler for a statue object. When a player or actor inspects the statue the handler prepares and displays a short descriptive text and manages related object state around that presentation.
